# 7 pre-existing grid unit failures: Pooling/Teleportation deltas + LockedColumns sync

## Context

Surfaced formally during PR #12938's review (the body claimed suite-green from a 41-test collection that didn't include these; the reviewer's exact-command run showed 46 passed / 7 failed). Control attribution 2026-06-12 ~01:40Z: the identical 7 fail on **dev tip with no feature branch** — pre-existing. They match the "7 pre-existing grid spec failures, flagged unowned" record from the `#12892`-era session (2026-06-08 window), so they predate this week's drag work.

`Release classification:` boardless pending triage — if any encodes a real regression in shipped behavior it escalates to the board; if they encode stale expectations from the multi-body migration they get rewritten.

## The failures (exact set)

- `LockedColumns.spec.mjs` — "Header Toolbar items are synchronized with column collection order"; "ScrollManager scroll pinning addon is updated on lock state change"
- `Pooling.spec.mjs` — "Vertical Scroll should recycle rows with ZERO structural deltas" (updates.length expected >0, got 0); "Scrolling to end of store should clamp correctly" (rowIndex 999 component undefined); "Performance Audit: Exact delta count for single row scroll"
- `Teleportation.spec.mjs` — "Scrolling should generate correct replacement deltas, not appends"; "Scrolling with Race Conditions (Async Updates) should not duplicate nodes"

Failure shape note: several are "nothing happened" (zero updates where scroll should produce them) rather than wrong-value — suggesting the test fixtures' scroll plumbing detached from the current multi-body/scrollbar-SSOT architecture rather than the engine silently breaking (the live probes exercise the same paths green). Triage must distinguish stale-fixture from real-regression per test.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each of the 7 attributed: stale-expectation (rewrite) vs real regression (escalate + fix).
- [ ] Suite green or the remaining reds are individually ticketed.

## Related

- Surfaced by: PR #12938 review (close-target/evidence honesty gate) · Era: the multi-body architecture window (#9486 family / #12892 trail)

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ❋ ❋ ## Triage complete — all 7 attributed, zero real regressions (AC1)
> 
> Lane claimed 02:36Z (was unassigned). Method: baseline reproduction on clean dev tip `c3d372054` (identical 7 fail; 34 siblings green), then per-spec source+history investigation with green sibling specs as comparators, then **independent adversarial verification of every verdict** (each verifier instructed to refute; **0/7 refuted**). Full evidence anchors below are file:line at `c3d372054`.
> 
> ### The cross-cutting mechanism (5 of 7)
> 
> Every Pooling/Teleportation failure shares ONE dead driver: the specs scroll via `body.scrollTop = N`, but `286a8db10` ("Finalize Grid Multi-Body Row Pooling & Vertical Sync", `#9488`, 2026-03-31) deliberately gutted `afterSetScrollTop` into a passive mirror — in-code comment: *"Controlled externally by Grid.Container.syncBodies()"* (`src/grid/Body.mjs:539-541`). The live path is DOM scroll → `ScrollManager.onContainerScroll` → throttled `syncGridBody` → `Container.syncBodies` (`Container.mjs:1217`) → `View.syncBodies` (`View.mjs:176-200`, later moved there by `96da8a981` / `#12757`). Repo-wide grep: **no shipped code writes `body.scrollTop` expecting render behavior — only these specs.** The tests' scrolls do literally nothing, which is exactly the observed "zero deltas / undefined row component" failure shapes. The asserted CONTRACTS (zero structural deltas, clamping, no duplication under racing updates) remain the engine's documented contracts (`View.mjs:166-174`) and are exercised green by `StoreInteractions.spec.mjs` via its store-mutation driver.
> 
> ⚠️ Verifier-flagged coverage gap: **no unit spec currently drives `syncBodies` at all** — scroll-axis recycle/race coverage is zero until the re-plumb lands. The rewrites below ADD missing coverage, not just repair it.
> 
> ### Verdict matrix
> 
> | # | Test | Verdict | Culprit | Disposition |
> |---|---|---|---|---|
> | 1 | LockedColumns :: header toolbar sync | **stale-expectation** | `36b45d700` (`#9487`) + `d04bc04fe` (`#12800`) | Spec asserts the pre-multi-body single-toolbar contract; engine correctly partitions into headerStart/headerToolbar/headerEnd (`header/Wrapper.mjs:84-108`, `Container.mjs:774-776,998-1030`). Received `['col1','col5']` = exactly the center region in collection order. Rewrite: three-region assertions + cross-region concat-equals-collection invariant + post-lock re-home (`headerStart === ['col3','col4','col1']`). |
> | 2 | LockedColumns :: scroll pinning addon | **stale-expectation** | `95d736724` (`#9490`) | `updateColumnScrollPinningAddon` + the whole `GridColumnScrollPinning` addon were DELETED (obsolete under multi-body region sub-grids) — the `TypeError: reading 'bind'` is a mock of a nonexistent API. Rewrite against the successor contract (`updateGridRowHoverSyncAddon` firing with `active === hasLockedColumns`, or structural: `bodyStart` exists + headerStart owns the locked column per `Container.mjs:802-855`). |
> | 3 | Pooling :: ZERO structural deltas | **broken-fixture-plumbing** | `286a8db10` (+ `96da8a981`) | Dead driver. Re-plumb: `grid.view.syncBodies(400)` + `view.promiseUpdate()`; keep ALL assertions (startIndex math unchanged → should pass). |
> | 4 | Pooling :: clamp at store end | **broken-fixture-plumbing** | `286a8db10` | Dead driver; clamping math itself predicts a pass post-re-plumb. If it still fails THEN, that's a real clamping regression — current failure carries zero clamping signal. |
> | 5 | Pooling :: exact delta count | **broken-fixture-plumbing** | `286a8db10` | Dead driver + stale calibration: the 1-row/6-cell/7-total constants were calibrated against the per-body update path (`#9019` era, 2026-02-06); current math (`Body.mjs:1487-1519`) recycles TWO rows for the 160px jump and deltas arrive via one batched View-level update. Keep zero-structural assertion; re-derive counts empirically. |
> | 6 | Teleportation :: replacement deltas | **broken-fixture-plumbing** | `286a8db10` | Dead driver (rowComponent undefined = scroll never happened). Re-plumb via `grid.syncBodies(scrollAmount)`; keep structural invariants strict; re-derive magic count 14. |
> | 7 | Teleportation :: race conditions | **broken-fixture-plumbing** | `286a8db10` | Dead driver; the only engine-reaching action is `bufferRowRange = 0` (prune re-render), explaining the entered-but-zero-textUpdates shape. Re-plumb as back-to-back `syncBodies(800); syncBodies(840)` (no await between — preserves the race intent); re-derive count 8; delete the verbatim-duplicated assertion block (`:329-331` ≡ `:338-340`, single-body-era copy-paste debt). |
> 
> ### AC2 path
> 
> Suite-green via rewrite, starting now in this lane (branch `agent/12941-grid-unit-replumb`): tests 1-2 assert documented successor contracts; tests 3-7 switch to the `syncBodies` driver with empirical recalibration of exact-count assertions. Escalation tripwire honored: if any re-plumbed test STILL fails on the contract itself (not the driver), it gets escalated as a real regression with its own leaf instead of being rewritten into silence.
>
